It is native to South Africa and is a member of the genus Catabrosa, which contains several species of grasses. The species was first discovered in the Drakensberg Mountains and is considered to be an endemic species to that region. Catabrosa drakensbergensis is typically classified as a monocot, as it belongs to the monophyletic clade of plants that have one cotyledon in their seeds. This classification is based on its anatomical and morphological characteristics, as well as its genetic relationships with other plant species.

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body.
